title: Phase 2A: ferrotorch-core::masked module via ferray-ma (torch.masked parity)
description:
Wire ferray-ma (masked arrays) into a new ferrotorch-core::masked module. Closes the torch.masked top-level gap flagged in docs/audit/README.md.

ferray-ma exposes (per its src layout): masked_array, mask_ops, arithmetic, manipulation, constructors, filled, extras (mvoid/nomask/masked_singleton/etc.), interop (numpy compat), io.

Scope:
- Add ferray-ma = workspace dep in ferrotorch-core/Cargo.toml.
- Define MaskedTensor<T> = (Tensor<T>, Tensor<bool>) struct or wrapper-with-mask in ferrotorch-core/src/masked/mod.rs. Mirrors torch.masked.MaskedTensor.
- Provide constructors (masked_tensor, as_masked), arithmetic (add/mul/etc passing mask), reductions (masked_sum/mean honoring mask), get_data / get_mask accessors.
- Provide fill_value protocol matching ferray-ma (default_fill_value, set_fill_value, common_fill_value).
- Re-export at ferrotorch-core::lib.rs.
- Tests: masked sum equals partial sum of unmasked elements; masked add propagates the mask intersection; round-trip with from_array / to_array.

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline before claiming done):
- bool tensors are not currently a first-class element type in ferrotorch (only f32/f64/bf16 per Float trait). Decide:
  (a) Use Tensor<u8> (0/1) as the mask — cheap, works on GPU today.
  (b) Block this issue on integer/bool element-type support (ferray-core change, separate effort).
- Forbidden: silently routing GPU MaskedTensor compute through host. ferray-ma is CPU-only ndarray, so any reduction over a GPU MaskedTensor MUST go through a GPU kernel that respects the mask, OR return DeviceUnavailable with a clear message. Do NOT call t.to(Cpu); op; t.to(Cuda) sandwich.
- Phase 2A scope: implement CPU-only with explicit DeviceUnavailable for GPU. Track GPU-native masked kernels (where_cond + masked_select already exist on GPU) as a follow-up issue.
- Verify ferrotorch-gpu has where_cond / masked_select kernels — if so, masked sum/mean can be implemented on GPU via where_cond + sum.

This is one of the few top-level torch.* surfaces with no current ferrotorch home. Phase 1 of the integration audit recommended it.
